title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. scapiforme
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. scapiforme, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. scapiforme include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for external use, and its leaves and roots can be used fresh or dried for herbal preparations.
